log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, likely a malware spreader with persistence capabilities, and has been analyzed in multiple sandboxes.
The sample has dropped a large number of files and has persistence capabilities through registry keys, but no network activity or process creation was observed.
The sample created a new process, wahiax.exe, and loaded various system modules, but no mutexes were created and no notable API calls were observed.
The sample has dropped a large number of files, including system files, executables, and text files, but the purpose and functionality of these files are unclear.
The sample has persistence capabilities through two registry keys, which point to the executables rxkbx.exe and ijjmk.exe, respectively.
The CAPA sandbox detected 5 MITRE ATT&CK techniques and 17 signature matches, but no network traffic or memory dumps were captured.
